question: Which project has the most issues?
reference_answer: microsoft/winget-pkgs with 60 issues
notes: Simple aggregation - SQL has clear advantage

question: Are there any duplicate project names (same repo name under different owners)? If so, which name is most common?
reference_answer: Yes, there are many duplicate project names. The most common is 'dotfiles' with 227 repos using that name.
notes: Simple aggregation with HAVING clause

question: What percentage of pull requests are merged?
reference_answer: 41.8% of pull requests are merged
notes: Simple aggregation

question: How many repos are there from vercel?
reference_answer: 10 repos from vercel (ai, ai-chatbot, commerce, hyper, next.js, nft, storage, swr, turbo, vercel)
notes: Simple count by owner

question: Which organization has the most repositories in the dataset?
reference_answer: microsoft has 92 repositories, followed by ministryofjustice (78), apache (70), openshift (39), and dotnet (27)
notes: Simple GROUP BY on owner

question: How many issues mention 'security' or 'vulnerability'? Which types of projects have the most security-related issues?
reference_answer: 1,811 issues mention security or vulnerability. Many are from dependency update bots (like dependabot) flagging security updates in various projects.
notes: Combines counting with content analysis

question: Find all open bug issues that mention a workaround in their body. List the repo and issue title.
reference_answer: There are 4 open bug issues with workarounds: quarto-dev/quarto-cli #1615 (Can't render README.qmd), Expensify/App #17588 (archive workspace rooms), Expensify/App #18891 (character limit), and more.
notes: Requires combining label parsing (semi-structured) with text search

question: Find issues that describe regression bugs (where something used to work but stopped working). What repos have these regression issues?
reference_answer: metabase/metabase has a regression issue (#14504) about CSV exports being truncated - it mentions this is a 'regression since 0.36.8'. dotnet/SqlClient also has regression-like issues.
notes: Requires understanding issue content to identify regression patterns

question: Which repositories have the most unique issue reporters (excluding bots)? List the top 5 with counts.
reference_answer: openshift org has 80 unique reporters, microsoft has 79, huggingface has 60, elastic has 57, and dotnet has 47 unique human issue reporters.
notes: Requires filtering out bots and counting distinct authors

question: Which human user (not a bot) has opened issues in the most different repositories?
reference_answer: Excluding bots, users like harmeetsingh11, fblackburn1, mikereiddigital, NiclasNorin, and Velin92 have opened issues across 3-5 different repositories each.
notes: Requires filtering bots and cross-referencing issues with repos

question: Which repositories have pull requests but have never merged any of them? List at least 5.
reference_answer: Several repos have 0% merge rate: noflo/noflo-nodejs (11 PRs), odoo/odoo (18 PRs), googleworkspace/node-samples (28 PRs), kumahq/kuma (13 PRs), microsoft/winget-pkgs (19 PRs).
notes: Requires finding absence of merged PRs

question: The Expensify/App repo has several issues marked as 'HOLD' in their title. What issue numbers are they waiting on? Look at the issue bodies to find the blocking issue references.
reference_answer: Expensify/App has multiple HOLD issues. Issue #11515 is on hold for #15212 (navigation improvements). Issue #17588 is also waiting for #15212. Issue #18891 is on hold for #18647. Issue #27937 is waiting for #27198.
notes: Requires reading issue bodies to extract referenced issue numbers

question: Which repositories have PRs that explicitly reference fixing a specific issue number (using 'Fixes #', 'Closes #', or 'Resolves #')? List the repo and what issues the PRs claim to fix.
reference_answer: sparklemotion/nokogiri has a PR fixing #350 (CSS :has() selector). r-lib/roxygen2 has a PR closing #1409 (importFrom error). scipy/scipy has a PR closing #16131. SteamDatabase/FileDetectionRuleSets has a PR closing #307.
notes: Requires parsing PR bodies to extract issue references

question: Find issues that contain detailed reproduction steps (with 'Steps to Reproduce', 'Expected', and 'Actual' sections). What common problems do these well-documented bug reports describe?
reference_answer: duplicati/duplicati has a well-documented issue about file access problems - it includes version info, steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior, and detailed error logs showing 'Access denied' errors.
notes: Requires reading and understanding issue structure and content

question: Looking at the dotnet/maui repository, what are the main categories of bugs being reported? Summarize the common themes across multiple issues.
reference_answer: dotnet/maui issues cover several themes: UI rendering problems (shadows slow on Android, Label FormattedText issues, RoundRectangle borders), platform-specific bugs (iOS, Android, MacCatalyst), performance issues (CollectionView, FilePicker slowness), and control-specific bugs (Switch, ImageButton, Grid styles).
notes: Requires reading multiple issues from same repo and synthesizing patterns

question: Which repository has the most diverse community engagement? Find the repo where issues get comments from the most unique users (excluding the issue author).
reference_answer: Expensify/App has the most community engagement with 28 unique commenters on issues (excluding issue authors), followed by home-assistant/core (21), elastic/kibana (13), llvm/llvm-project (11), and microsoft/vscode (11).
notes: Requires joining issues, comments, filtering out self-comments

question: Find issues that report memory leaks or out-of-memory errors. What applications or systems are affected?
reference_answer: Memory-related issues include: microsoft/vscode #43226 (memory in extension API), joltup/rn-fetch-blob #24 (crash downloading large files), kedacore/charts #226 (helm chart issues), chartjs/Chart.js #10890 (chart shrinking with zoom).
notes: Requires semantic understanding of memory-related problems

question: Which pull requests mention 'breaking changes'? Were these PRs merged? What kind of breaking changes were they?
reference_answer: Several PRs mention breaking changes, mostly dependency updates. NixOS/nixpkgs #224796 was merged (adding libusbp). Most breaking change PRs from bots (like dependabot updating less-loader to v11, uuid to v9) were NOT merged.
notes: Requires understanding PR content and correlating with merge status

question: Find issues where someone reported a bug, and then later someone submitted a PR that claims to fix that specific issue. List these bug-fix pairs.
reference_answer: Examples include: sparklemotion/nokogiri issue #350 fixed by a PR implementing CSS :has() selector, r-lib/roxygen2 issue #1409 about importFrom error has a closing PR, scipy issue #16131 about spsolve_triangular performance has a fixing PR.
notes: Requires correlating issues and PRs through reference parsing

question: Which issues have labels that seem contradictory (like both 'bug' and 'enhancement')? List the repos and issue titles.
reference_answer: Issues with both bug and enhancement labels: BradyAJohnston/MolecularNodes #120 (Support .MOL files), UtilitechAS/amsreader-firmware #627 (device reboots), LeavesMC/Leaves #134, arihanv/MiniStream #3 (entire app broken), TTT-2/TTT2 #1275 (Crosshair fixes).
notes: Requires parsing labels and understanding semantic conflict

question: Identify the 'bus factor' risk: find repositories where a single user accounts for more than 50% of the pull requests. Who are these key contributors?
reference_answer: Many repositories have high bus factor risk. Look for repos where authors like dependabot[bot] or renovate[bot] dominate PR activity, or where single human contributors like oliver-jenny, Seong-Hoon-Lim, or daminoworld contribute most PRs to their repos.
notes: Requires calculating per-author contribution percentages

question: Which issues have stack traces or error logs in their body? Categorize them by programming language/platform based on the error format.
reference_answer: Issues with stack traces: duplicati/duplicati (C#/.NET exceptions), zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr (Python ImportError), joltup/rn-fetch-blob (React Native crash), home-assistant/core (Python), scrapy/scrapy (Python async errors), elastic/elasticsearch-dsl-py (Python connection errors).
notes: Requires pattern recognition across different language stack trace formats

question: Find the longest issue descriptions (by character count). What makes these issues so detailed? Summarize what they're about.
reference_answer: The longest issues are: MihuBot/runtime-utils #220 (100k+ chars, detailed implementation for getStaticFieldCurrentClass), and several dependabot PRs with massive dependency update descriptions (~65k chars each) in repos like zhuxiaow0/calico, paketo-buildpacks/dotnet-core-aspnet-runtime.
notes: Requires reading and summarizing very long content

question: Which events show the most activity in the dataset? What types of GitHub events are captured and what's the distribution?
reference_answer: The dataset has 100k events. Distribution: PushEvent (63,659), CreateEvent (10,739), PullRequestEvent (7,100), IssueCommentEvent (4,193), WatchEvent (3,518), DeleteEvent (3,014), PullRequestReviewEvent (2,592), IssuesEvent (1,426).
notes: Simple aggregation on events table

question: Which human user (excluding bots) has made the most comments across all issues and PRs?
reference_answer: Among humans, liferay-continuous-integration made 48 comments across 16 threads. Other active commenters include breathx (41 comments), wingetbot (38), qperie (33), and openshift-ci-robot (34). Note: some of these may be CI systems rather than humans.
notes: Requires filtering bots and aggregating comments

question: Find issues that reference 'depends on', 'blocked by', or 'waiting for' another issue. What blocking relationships exist?
reference_answer: Issues with dependencies: haskell/cabal #4035 (private dependencies), bazelbuild/bazel #11523 (private git repos), kedacore/charts #226 (CRD installation order), openshift/cluster-node-tuning-operator #484 (Hypershift adaptation).
notes: Requires text parsing to find dependency relationships

question: Which repositories have issues labeled as 'good first issue' that are still open? How many are there per repo?
reference_answer: Repos with open 'good first issue' labels: AnupK1234/MealMatters (2), hasadna/open-bus-map-search (2), and several repos with 1 each including SleepAPI/SleepAPI, chaoxel/PixelPal, pooranjoyb/StoryBook-RN.
notes: Requires parsing JSON labels field

question: Compare the llvm/llvm-project and NixOS/nixpkgs repositories. Which has more active contributors? How do their issue and PR counts compare?
reference_answer: llvm/llvm-project has 25 unique issue authors and 26 PR authors. NixOS/nixpkgs has 16 issue authors but 26 PR authors. NixOS/nixpkgs has significantly more issues (688) compared to llvm/llvm-project. Both have strong community contribution.
notes: Requires comparing metrics across two specific repos

question: Which issues have been open the longest without being closed? What repos are they in and what are they about?
reference_answer: The dataset spans from 2011 to 2024. The oldest open issues date back to early dates in the dataset. Look for issues with created_at dates from 2011-2015 that still have state='open'.
notes: Requires temporal analysis of issue age

question: Are there any users who both reported an issue AND submitted a PR to fix their own issue? List them and their self-fixes.
reference_answer: dependabot[bot] leads with 208 self-fixes. Among humans: oliver-jenny has 9 self-fixed issues, Seong-Hoon-Lim and daminoworld have 4 each, Manav-Aggarwal has 3.
notes: Requires joining issues and PRs on author and matching issue references in PR body
